title: epic: jmap-chat crate API idiomatics cleanup
description:
## Purpose
Address the idiomatic Rust issues surfaced in the post-phase-2 API review. These are not correctness bugs in the protocol implementation — they are API design and type-system issues that would catch a Rust bigot's eye in a code review.

## Scope
All findings are in the jmap-chat library crate. None require protocol spec changes.

## Issues (priority order)
- jmap-chat-tui-rlvt (P1 bug): Patch::Keep silently serializes as null in release builds
- jmap-chat-tui-dzg8 (P2): split AuthProvider transport from credential injection
- jmap-chat-tui-s4q2 (P2): store base_url as url::Url instead of String
- jmap-chat-tui-y9se (P3): remove Deref<Target=str> from Id and UTCDate newtypes
- jmap-chat-tui-37s2 (P4): rename from_trusted to from_raw
- jmap-chat-tui-764r (P4): From<Option<T>> for Patch<T> None→Keep semantics
- jmap-chat-tui-mrr9 (P4): call_batch type erasure — reconsider API

## Design notes
The P1 bug (Patch::Keep) should be fixed first as it is a latent correctness issue in release builds. The P2 refactors (AuthProvider split, base_url type) are independent and can proceed in parallel. P3/P4 items are style and ergonomics improvements that carry no urgency.
close_reason: All 7 child issues implemented and two review rounds passed (P0=P1=P2=0). Tests: 259 passing, 0 failing.
